mnemonics: ND-Arrays Are Like LEGOs: NumPy's core is N-Dimensional arrays. Think of building blocks (LEGOs) that are all the same size and type, stacked in N directions to form a structure (an array).
keyPoints: NumPy arrays are homogeneous, meaning all elements must be of the same data type, leading to efficient storage and computation.
The shape of an array defines its structure (e.g., (3, 4) for a 3-row, 4-column 2D array), and the ndim attribute reveals its number of dimensions.
Vectorized operations allow you to perform computations on entire arrays without writing explicit loops, which is the key to NumPy's speed.
commonMistakes: Assuming standard Python list operations (like using '+' to concatenate) work the same way on NumPy arrays, when they often perform element-wise or mathematical operations instead.
Forgetting that array indexing returns a view (not a copy) of the original data, so modifying the view also modifies the original array, which can lead to unintended side-effects.
Attempting to create an array with inconsistent data types (e.g., mixing integers and strings) and not realizing NumPy will forcibly convert them all to a single, common type (like strings).
